phase1.output: (Window with interactive components showing real-time updates via binding)
phase1.explanation:
Observable properties and binding make UIs reactive:

Observable Properties:
• StringProperty — for text
• IntegerProperty — for integers
• DoubleProperty — for decimals
• BooleanProperty — for true/false
• ObjectProperty — for any object

Creating properties:
• new SimpleStringProperty("initial")
• new SimpleIntegerProperty(0)

Getting/Settings:
• get() — returns value
• set(value) — updates value
• getValue() / setValue() — for property types

Binding Types:

1. Unidirectional Binding:
   • target.bind(source)
   • target updates when source changes
   • Example: label.textProperty().bind(field.textProperty())

2. Bidirectional Binding:
   • property.bindBidirectional(otherProperty)
   • Both properties stay in sync
   • Changes in one update the other

3. Numeric Bindings:
   • Bindings.add(a, b)
   • Bindings.multiply(a, b)
   • Custom: Bindings.createStringBinding()

4. Boolean Bindings:
   • property.not() — negation
   • property.and(other) — AND
   • property.or(other) — OR

Listeners:
• addListener((obs, oldVal, newVal) -> { ... })
• Called when value changes
• Use for side effects

Benefits:
• Automatic UI updates
• Clean, declarative code
• Reduced boilerplate
• Reactive programming style

⚠️ Bindings can cause memory leaks if not handled properly
⚠️ Avoid circular bindings

phase2.errorMessage:
Test.java:12: error: incompatible types: TextField cannot be converted to ObservableValue<String>
        label.textProperty().bind(field);  // ERROR!
                             ^
1 error
phase2.explanation:
You need to bind to the textProperty() of the TextField!

Wrong: label.textProperty().bind(field);
Correct: label.textProperty().bind(field.textProperty());

Always bind to properties, not the control itself.

Common bindable properties:
• textProperty() — TextField, Label
• valueProperty() — Slider, ProgressBar
• selectedProperty() — CheckBox, RadioButton

phase3.prompt:
Write a JavaFX program with:
1. A Slider (0-100, default 50)
2. A ProgressBar that shows the slider value (0.0 to 1.0)
3. A Label that displays the exact value

Use property binding to connect all three components.

Hint: ProgressBar expects 0.0-1.0, Slider gives 0-100.
dialogueHints: Or use createStringBinding for custom formatting
